title: Counting Unit Cubes to Find Volume

steps.work: Volume is the amount of space a 3D shape takes up, measured in cubic units.
steps.explanation: Just as area measures flat space, volume measures 3D space.

steps.instruction: A rectangular prism is 4 cubes long, 3 cubes wide, and 2 cubes tall. Count the volume.
steps.work: Bottom layer: 4 × 3 = 12 cubes. There are 2 layers. Total: 12 × 2 = 24 cubes.
steps.explanation: Counting by layers: find the number of cubes in one layer, then multiply by the number of layers.
Volume is always in cubic units: length × width × height.

title: Finding Volume of a Composite Shape

steps.instruction: An L-shaped solid is made from two rectangular prisms. Prism A: 3 × 2 × 2. Prism B: 2 × 2 × 2.
steps.work: The shape is not a simple rectangular prism, so we split it into parts.
steps.explanation: Composite shapes can be broken into simpler rectangular prisms.

steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of each prism.
steps.work: Prism A: 3 × 2 × 2 = 12 cubic units. Prism B: 2 × 2 × 2 = 8 cubic units.

steps.explanation: The total volume of the L-shape is the sum of its parts.
